description: This EBSD grain boundary map displays the microstructural evolution of Fe–8% Al low-density ferritic steel following hot torsion deformation, illustrating the dynamic recrystallization mechanisms under investigation. The image reveals a complex network of grain boundaries differentiated by color (red and black lines likely representing high-angle boundaries [HABs] and low-angle boundaries [LABs] respectively), with a scale bar indicating 500 μm. The microstructure shows an aggregate of crystallites with mixed boundary character, consistent with either the progressive transformation of subgrain boundaries into grain boundaries during Continuous Dynamic Recrystallization (CDRX) or the mixture of new equiaxed grains and elongated parent grains characteristic of Discontinuous Dynamic Recrystallization (DDRX). The directional arrows indicate the deformation axis or shear direction relative to the microstructural features. This figure provides visual evidence for grain structure evolution during high-temperature deformation (900–1100°C) at strain rates of 0.1–10 s⁻¹, supporting the paper's conclusions regarding the competition between CDRX and DDRX mechanisms and their distinct effects on grain morphology and boundary misorientation distributions.
